module-1-discount-audit-report.label: Module 1: Discount Audit Report
module-1-discount-audit-report.summary: Calculate gross sales, discount amount, and net sales while clearly labeling rows that need review.

project_step_1.title: Calculate Gross Sales
project_step_1.prompt: Start the module project. In step 1 of 3, Write a query to calculate the gross sales for each order in the sales_reporting table.
project_step_1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

project_step_1.solutionCode:
SELECT order_id, unit_price * quantity AS gross_sales
FROM sales_reporting;

project_step_2.title: Calculate Discount Amount
project_step_2.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 3, Write a query to calculate the discount amount for each order, using COALESCE to handle NULL discount percentages.
project_step_2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

project_step_3.title: Calculate Net Sales
project_step_3.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 3, Write a query to calculate the net sales for each order, subtracting the discount amount from gross sales.
project_step_3.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

       COALESCE(discount_pct, 0) / 100 * (unit_price * quantity) AS discount_amount
FROM sales_reporting;

sketch-1.title: Understanding NULL in Calculations
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, NULL represents a missing or undefined value. When performing arithmetic operations, any calculation involving NULL results in NULL. For example, if you try to calculate the total price with a NULL discount, the result will be NULL. To handle this, we use the COALESCE function to provide a default value when NULL is encountered.

```sql
SELECT order_id, unit_price * quantity AS gross_sales,
       COALESCE(discount_pct, 0) AS discount_pct
FROM sales_reporting;
```

In this example, COALESCE replaces NULL discount percentages with 0, ensuring calculations proceed without interruption.
